title: Phase 3.4.6 — Personal command wrapper at ~/.local/bin/<name>
description: Generate ~/.local/bin/<name> wrapper + ensure PATH on install, remove on uninstall. Spec: .planning/phases/phase-3-tasks/3.4.6-personal-cmd.md

value: Four non-negotiable architectural invariants (from CLAUDE.md). Breaking requires ADR + explicit operator approval, not just a PR. 1) Engine-agnostic core: only lib/engines/<id>.sh references Claude-specific stuff (binary 'claude', ~/.claude/, Claude tool names). install.sh, lib/*.sh outside engines/, src/, tests/ must stay generic. 2) Clean uninstall: uninstall.sh leaves the system as if Claudify never ran. Everything under ~/.claudify-<name>/ or ~/.config/systemd/user/. No /etc, no /var, no env-var pollution, no orphan PATH entries. 3) Substrate independence: memory/persona/conversation data lives in plain files + SQLite at known paths. Operator can inspect/edit/backup with cat/sqlite3/vi even with every Claudify process dead. MCP/skills are glue, not gates. 4) Single-user trust model: no malicious-skill defenses, no per-skill sandboxing, no permission systems. Isolation is for accident prevention, not adversaries.

Mount-namespace isolation (ProtectHome=tmpfs + BindPaths) is PERMANENTLY ABANDONED. Station11 testing on 2026-05-10 proved AppArmor on Ubuntu 24.04 blocks unprivileged userns mount operations — directives parse, systemctl show reports them, but /proc/<pid>/ns/mnt shows no namespace was created. 3.6.2 spec marked SUPERSEDED; original 45-min task reduced to 5-min cleanup (strip the directives so the unit doesn't claim isolation it can't deliver). Real cross-instance isolation lives in 3.4.9 containerize (kernel-enforced namespaces). See ADR 0006 appendix.

Per ADR 0006 (2026-05-11): per-instance layout is FLAT (~/.claudify-<name>/) NOT nested (~/.claudify/instances/<n>/). Side-car registry at ~/.claudify-registry.json. Each instance is fully self-contained at its top-level dir. Older docs/specs (especially Phase 4-7) may still reference the nested layout — update the prose, not the code (code uses $CLAUDIFY_INSTANCE_DIR which resolves correctly under either form). Service name: claudify-<name>.service. CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR points at <instance>/claude/ for per-instance Claude state.

For non-interactive client install (round-trip tests, automation): pre-seed three files BEFORE running install with --preserve-state --non-interactive, so secrets never appear in process argv. Paths: ~/.claudify-<name>/credentials.env (chmod 600, line: CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN=sk-ant-oat01-...), ~/.claudify-<name>/channels/telegram/.env (chmod 600, line: TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN=...), ~/.claudify-<name>/channels/telegram/access.json (chmod 644, JSON with dmPolicy/allowFrom/groups/pending). Then bash install.sh --name <n> --preserve-state --non-interactive.

Phase 3 status as of 2026-05-12: 8 of 13 sub-tasks DONE. Shipped (with dates): 3.1 uninstall (04-23), 3.2 update.sh + --preserve-state (04-24), 3.3 starter CLAUDE.md persona (04-24), 3.4.1 repo skeleton (04-28), 3.4.2 lib/steps.sh split (04-30), 3.4.2.1 resume install (05-04), 3.4.3 engine abstraction (05-04), 3.4.4 manifest files (05-04), 3.4.5 multi-instance flat layout + Tier-1 hardening (05-11), 3.4.5.1 skill data substrate + lib/memory.sh (05-12), 3.4.5.2 engine contract 8→10 fns (05-12). Remaining in Phase 3: 3.4.6 personal cmd (~45m), 3.4.7 migration (~30m), 3.4.8 docs sync (~30m), 3.4.9 containerize (~7hr), 3.5 backup/restore TS (~3-4hr). Then Phase 4 security.

Phase numbering reorg on 2026-05-12. Pipeline now: Phase 3 (lifecycle) → Phase 4 (security, was 3.6.x) → Phase 5 (memory MCP, was 4.0a/0b/1/1.1/4) → Phase 6 (skills, was 4.2/3/5/6) → Phase 7 (observability, was Phase 5). Renumber map: 3.6.X→4.X, 4.0a→5.0a, 4.0b→5.0b, 4.1→5.1, 4.1.1→5.1.1, 4.4→5.2, 4.2→6.1, 4.3→6.2, 4.5→6.3, 4.6→6.4. Old numbers still appear in commits before 5ec3dde. Each new phase has its own folder under .planning/phases/ with README.md status board.

Skill data substrate (Phase 3.4.5.1, shipped 2026-05-12) — every skill gets a private data dir at ~/.claudify-<name>/data/<skill-id>/ (mode 700, persists across update.sh + --reset-config). Operator-facing env var: ${CLAUDIFY_SKILL_DATA} matches Anthropic's ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA} so cross-ecosystem skills work. lib/memory.sh exposes 5 helpers: memory_dir, memory_path, memory_assert_write, memory_assert_read, memory_export_env. Asserts are accident-prevention (typo guard for db-names), not a sandbox — single-user trust per ADR 0006. Manifest gained skills[].memory.{writes,reads} schema; writes/reads accept string OR array form. manifest_set_skill + manifest_get_skill_memory are the helpers.

lib/engines/claude-code.sh is 344 lines (above the CLAUDE.md ≤300 budget) on PURPOSE. The 'no cross-adapter sourcing' architectural invariant means the 10-function engine contract has to live in one file per engine — splitting it would break the invariant. Header comment in the file + .planning/phases/phase-3-tasks/3.4.5.2-summary.md document the exception. Don't 'fix' it by splitting.

Station11 test server: ssh -i ~/.ssh/station11 -p 43532 david@77.42.21.117. Use for all round-trip tests. Working dir on server: /home/david. To run user systemctl commands over ssh, export XDG_RUNTIME_DIR=/run/user/$(id -u) first.

.planning/LOCAL-autoinstall.sh (laptop template) and ~/test/autoinstall.sh (Station11 copy) are STALE — written pre-3.4.5, still reference service name 'claude-telegram', path '~/.claudify/' (nested), and EnvironmentFile paths from the old single-instance layout. Patch before reuse: service name 'claudify-<name>', path '~/.claudify-<name>/' (flat per ADR 0006). Burns 10+ min if forgotten.

Engine adapter contract is 10 functions (since 3.4.5.2 on 2026-05-12, was 8). Defined in lib/engines/<id>.sh — implement all 10, can be no-ops. The 10: engine_install, engine_seed_state <wsdir>, engine_install_channel_plugin <name>, engine_auth_check, engine_auth_setup, engine_run_args, engine_status, engine_uninstall, engine_memory_setup (no-op stub today; Phase 5.0b fills with 'claude mcp add claudify-memory ...'), engine_apply_persona <text> (marker-bracketed write into <instance>/workspace/CLAUDE.md, idempotent). Today's only adapter: claude-code.sh. New adapter (Gemini/Codex/etc.) = drop a new lib/engines/<id>.sh implementing all 10.
